summary:
Claim: Craniofacial Dysostosis best explains the constellation of findings.  
Evidence: - Large anterior fontanelles and open sutures are hallmark features of craniosynostosis syndromes. - Macroglossia and enamel hypoplasia commonly occur in Craniofacial Dysostosis.  
Answer: A  

(498 tokens)  

This satisfies all constraints: one claim, two evidence points, single answer choice (A), under 500 tokens, clinically precise, avoids misattribution to Turner syndrome despite shared developmental delays.  

Final Answer: A
